description:
Until this atom, every fz_fn_<id> has signature `extern "C" fn(*mut u8, *mut u8) -> *mut u8` — the uniform trampoline ABI. After VR.3 the frame slots are typed but the call boundary still goes through the frame.

Direct calls to monomorphic fns (Term::Call with statically-resolved callee and typed args) now use a native ABI matching the callee's typed signature: `extern "C" fn add1(n: i64) -> i64`. Result returns via register; no frame allocation per direct call.

Indirect calls (Term::CallClosure, polymorphic dispatch through a Var) keep the uniform trampoline ABI. Closure invocation pays the boxing cost at the invoke site, not at every value op inside the callee.

## Adds

  - Per-fn Signature computation in compile()/compile_aot() based on the fn's typed entry-block params.
  - emit_call / emit_tail_call pick between native call (direct, monomorphic) and trampoline (indirect, polymorphic).
  - fn_sig in declare-functions loop becomes per-fn instead of one fixed signature for all.
  - Fixtures asserting direct calls don't allocate a frame.

## Kills

  - Frame-load-at-entry boilerplate: `load.i64 frame_ptr+24`, ..., `frame_ptr+24+8*N` at the top of every fn lowering. Direct-call fns now receive args via registers.
  - Frame-allocate-at-call boilerplate at Term::Call sites: `fz_alloc_frame_dyn(callee_id)` + slot writes. Direct-call sites use native Cranelift `call` insts with typed args.
  - The "every fz_fn_<N> has identical extern signature" assumption in fn_sig construction.
  - fz_alloc_frame_dyn calls from direct-call sites. The fn itself survives — still needed for CallClosure where callee isn't statically known.

## Acceptance

  - Direct calls between monomorphic fns in the fixture matrix don't allocate a heap frame (VR.0 assertion: no `fz_alloc_frame` symbol reference in the direct-call site's CLIF).
  - Matrix green.
  - cargo test green; heap_live_count for non-concurrent fixtures drops materially (record baseline before, diff after).
title: VR.4: per-fn native ABI for direct calls
